description: Remove the version property from the adapter IIFE and plugin class contract. The adapter hash is the real integrity check — the version check is redundant dead code. Remove verifyAdapterVersion and all version plumbing through the injection pipeline. Remove abstract version from OpenTabsPlugin. Keep PluginMeta.version (server-side, from package.json) for display purposes.

userStories.title: Remove verifyAdapterVersion and version plumbing from injection pipeline
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the injection pipeline to rely solely on the adapter hash check so that there is no redundant version verification that can produce misleading warnings.
acceptanceCriteria: verifyAdapterVersion function is deleted from platform/browser-extension/src/iife-injection.ts
The version parameter is removed from injectAdapterFile function signature
The version parameter is removed from injectPluginIntoMatchingTabs function signature
All call sites that passed version to these functions are updated (no more version argument)
injectPluginsIntoTab no longer passes plugin.version to injectAdapterFile
The adapter hash verification (verifyAdapterHash) remains unchanged — it is the real integrity check
The verifyAdapterVersion export is removed from the module's exported test surface
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
File: `platform/browser-extension/src/iife-injection.ts`

1. Delete the `verifyAdapterVersion` function (lines 38-64). It reads `globalThis.__openTabs.adapters[name].version` from the page and compares it to an expected version, but the adapter hash already covers this — if the IIFE content is correct, the hash matches; if it's stale, the hash catches it and retries.

2. Remove `version` parameter from `injectAdapterFile` (line 183) — change signature from `(tabId, pluginName, version?, adapterHash?, adapterFilePath?)` to `(tabId, pluginName, adapterHash?, adapterFilePath?)`. Remove the two `if (version)` blocks at lines 202-204 and 222-224.

3. Remove `version` parameter from `injectPluginIntoMatchingTabs` (line 332) — it currently has `version?: string` at line 332. Remove it and update the call to `injectAdapterFile` at line 358.

4. Update call sites:
   - `platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts` line 284: remove version arg from `injectPluginIntoMatchingTabs` call
   - `platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts` line 318: remove version arg
   - `platform/browser-extension/src/iife-injection.ts` line 412: `injectAdapterFile(tabId, plugin.name, plugin.version, ...)` → remove `plugin.version`
   - `platform/browser-extension/src/iife-injection.ts` line 499: same pattern, remove version arg

5. Remove `verifyAdapterVersion` from the module's exported test surface (line 521).

6. Update tests in `platform/browser-extension/src/iife-injection.test.ts` — remove the `verifyAdapterVersion` describe block (lines 161-197) and update any mock setup that references it. Also check `platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.test.ts` line 199 which mocks `verifyAdapterVersion`.

userStories.title: Remove abstract version from OpenTabsPlugin and from plugin classes
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the SDK to not require a version property on the plugin class so that the version comes solely from package.json.
acceptanceCriteria: OpenTabsPlugin no longer declares abstract readonly version: string in platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts
plugins/slack/src/index.ts no longer has readonly version = '0.0.1'
plugins/e2e-test/src/index.ts no longer has readonly version = '0.0.1'
The scaffold template in platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts no longer includes readonly version in generated plugin classes
The validatePlugin function in platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts no longer validates plugin.version (remove lines 320-327)
All related tests are updated to not reference plugin.version from the class
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
npm run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
Multiple files to edit:

1. `platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts` line 193-194: Remove `/** Semver version string */` and `abstract readonly version: string;`

2. `plugins/slack/src/index.ts` line 29: Remove `readonly version = '0.0.1';`

3. `plugins/e2e-test/src/index.ts` line 74: Remove `readonly version = '0.0.1';`

4. `platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts` line 357: Remove `  readonly version = '0.0.1';` from the template string in `generatePluginIndex()`.

5. `platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts` lines 320-327: Remove the version validation block from `validatePlugin()`. The package.json version is already validated by `parsePluginPackageJson()` — no need for the class to carry a version.

6. Tests to update:
   - `platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.test.ts`: Any tests that check version validation in validatePlugin need updating. Search for 'version' in test assertions.
   - `platform/cli/src/scaffold.test.ts`: May assert generated plugin code contains 'version'. Remove that assertion.
   - `platform/create-plugin/src/index.test.ts`: Similar — check around line 332.
   - `platform/browser-extension/src/tool-dispatch.test.ts` line 81: `version: '1.0.0'` in mock PluginMeta — keep this, PluginMeta still has version (from server-side package.json).
   - `platform/browser-extension/src/tab-state.test.ts` line 79, `tab-matching.test.ts` line 204, etc.: Same — these use PluginMeta.version which stays.

IMPORTANT: `PluginMeta.version` in the browser extension (from chrome.storage.local) is NOT removed. It comes from the server (which reads package.json) and is used for display in the side panel and CLI. Only the class-level abstract property and the injection pipeline version check are removed.

Also important: `platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts` `RegisteredPlugin.version` stays — it's the server-side version from package.json. `platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts` line 127 still sends `version: plugin.version` to the extension. The extension still stores it in PluginMeta. None of the server-side or storage version fields change — only the SDK abstract property and build-time validation are removed.
